jobs.title: Director of Development, Campaign Initiatives
jobs.description:
Job Summary

The Director of Development for Campaign Initiatives is a front-line fundraising professional responsible for developing and executing strategies to secure philanthropic support for select campaign priorities, multi-disciplinary projects, and non-college specific initiatives across San José State University. Reporting to the Associate Vice President for Development, the Director will provide strategic direction and vision for key fundraising priorities, develop prospect pipelines, and craft compelling cases for support for a variety of fundraising projects and programs. Campaign initiatives for which the incumbent will have primary fundraising responsibility include, but are not limited to, the preservation of Tower Hall, Speed City Legacy Track, Artificial Intelligence, and campaign initiatives from the Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr. Library.

The responsibilities include but are not limited to identifying, qualifying, cultivating, soliciting and stewarding individuals capable of making major and principal gifts to the University. In support of these efforts, the Director maintains and strengthens close working relationships with key academic leaders, campus administrators, various stakeholders and University Advancement colleagues to build consensus around complex funding opportunities.

Key Responsibilities

Manage an assigned portfolio of key prospects and individuals whose capacity and interests align with university-wide strategic initiatives and campaign priorities
Coordinate with Advancement Research and Prospect Research to proactively build and qualify a robust prospect pipeline
Develop and execute complex strategies to identify, cultivate, solicit and steward major gift prospects/donors, including joint solicitations on multi-unit projects
Present and regularly review top prospect strategies with immediate supervisor and other key team members as appropriate
Work collaboratively with university administrators, academic leadership and other stakeholders to gain counsel and offer expertise on donor-centric cultivation and solicitation strategy
Work collaboratively with peers across all development units (Major Gifts, Annual Giving, Planned Giving, Corporate & Foundation Relations) and serve as a key horizontal partner on multi-unit projects
Work with volunteer leadership of university boards, councils and committees to maximize cultivation and solicitation efforts
Focus on securing funding for designated priorities including but not limited to academic programs, endowed chairs and professorships, scholarships, unrestricted support, facility renovations, new facilities and other specified priorities
Collaborate with donor relations colleagues with stewardship efforts to promote a culture of gratitude among internal and external constituents
Develop comprehensive, persuasive proposals for complex, multi-unit funding opportunities, gathering data and buy-in from various campus stakeholder
Effectively utilize campus data management systems and enter prospect/donor contact reports and their relevant data in a timely fashion
Participate in staff, functional and strategy meetings including prospect review sessions, and attend conferences, seminars and other development training as feasible and appropriate

Knowledge, Skills & Abilities

Ability to identify donors and close gifts of six and seven figures
Ability to navigate the complexity of a large, dynamic university and establish consensus among varied stakeholders to make the appropriate match for donor interests
Skilled in networking, high-level prospect discovery and opening doors among new donor segments, coupled with the ability to design and implement sophisticated, donor-centric cultivation and solicitation strategies
Superior ability to successfully manage multiple, large-scale projects with evolving priorities on tight deadlines and under pressure in a dynamic, politically complex and open environment
Exceptional skill in defining problems, gathering and synthesizing complex data, establishing facts and drawing valid conclusions
Exceptional verbal communication and persuasive writing skills
High level of initiative, attention to detail, and organization skills
Proficient in Google Suite and Microsoft Office Suite, and ability to utilize fundraising database programs and learn new tech platforms for project management
Ability to communicate with constituents in a professional and respectful manner

Required Qualifications

Bachelor’s degree from an accredited institution
Minimum of three to five years of progressively responsible experience in fundraising
Experience developing and leading complex solicitation strategies resulting in closing major gifts
Evidence of superior written communication skills; ability to articulate and draft persuasive proposals

Preferred Qualifications

Advanced degree
Experience in identifying, qualifying, and building strategies for new prospects, particularly those with capacity for seven-figure gifts or higher
Fundraising experience within a comprehensive campaign setting at a research or public institution of higher education, specifically managing non-college or cross-disciplinary campaign priorities
Experience serving as a strategic partner to campus administrators and academic leaders

Compensation

Classification: Administrator II

Anticipated Hiring Range: $8,084/month - $10,000/month

CSU Salary Range: $5,053/month - $16,221/month

The final hiring salary will be commensurate with experience.

San José State University offers employees a comprehensive benefits package typically worth 30-35% of your base salary. For more information on programs available, please see the Employee Benefits Summary .

jobs.title: Managing Director, Teachers' Venture Growth - North America Direct Investing
jobs.description:
The Opportunity

Ontario Teachers’ Pension Plan (“Teachers’”) manages ~C$270B globally and is recognized as one of the most sophisticated direct investors in the world. With over 80% of assets managed internally, we combine long-term capital with disciplined underwriting and the ability to lead at scale. Teachers’ Venture Growth (TVG) is our dedicated late-stage venture and growth equity platform, managing ~C$15B globally. On the direct investment front, we deploy ~C$1B annually into minority investments in scaled private technology companies. TVG has invested in category leaders such as SpaceX, Databricks, Canva, Anthropic, Gusto, Grafana, Kong, StackAdapt, and many others.

We are seeking a Managing Director in San Francisco
to join our North America Direct team. We are intentionally looking to add a Silicon Valley-based investor who brings differentiated access, cycle-tested judgment, and the ambition to help scale a high-performing institutional growth platform.

The Mandate

You Will

Own sector coverage within North American technology
Develop differentiated investment theses
Originate, lead, and close minority growth investments (typically Series C +)
Write and defend investment committee recommendations
Serve as an active board member or observer
Develop junior talent and elevate team performance

TVG’s average initial equity commitment is ~US$100M and we frequently lead or co-lead rounds. You will collaborate closely with senior TVG leadership, the TVG Funds team, and adjacent OTPP asset classes, but investment decisions and portfolio ownership resides within TVG.

What Success Looks Like

Within the first 12–24 months, you will have:

Expanded our access to high-quality companies and founders not already in our network
Led and closed compelling later-stage investments
Demonstrated strong board engagement and value-add through inflection points
Raised the bar for analytical rigor and decision quality across the team
Developed junior investors through direct coaching and meaningful ownership

We are building the next phase of our North America platform and this role is expected to contribute materially to that evolution.

The Profile

We Are Seeking An Investor Who Brings

12+ years of direct investing experience in later-stage venture or growth equity
A demonstrated track record leading minority investments in scaled technology companies
Meaningful board experience and exposure through exit
Deep, credible relationships across Silicon Valley and the broader North American technology ecosystem
Strong transaction leadership and negotiation capabilities

We will not consider candidates whose experience is limited to early-stage investing or those without direct underwriting responsibility.

Equally Important

Experience investing through multiple market cycles with visible evidence of judgment shaped by volatility
High standards, intellectual independence, and accountability
A collaborative, strengths-based leadership style suited to a global institutional platform

We focus primarily on later-stage technology assets across a wide range of sectors, however, we are generally less active in consumer internet and health tech.

Why Teachers’

Teachers’ Offers Something Distinctive In Silicon Valley

Long-duration capital that allows us to invest through cycles
The ability to write large checks and lead
A disciplined, globally integrated investment culture
A truly global platform with deep institutional resources
A clear purpose: delivering retirement security to 340,000 teachers who rely on us for long-term financial stability in retirement

Our capital is not transient. It represents the deferred earnings of educators. That responsibility shapes how we underwrite risk, support companies, and build enduring value. This is an opportunity to combine institutional scale with entrepreneurial impact.

Compensation

Base salary range: USD $300,000–$380,000
, plus eligibility for Teachers’ annual incentive plan and Long Term Incentive Plan compensation programs. Final compensation reflects experience, capability, and internal alignment.

What We’re Offering

Numerous opportunities for professional growth and development
Comprehensive employer paid benefits coverage
A flexible work environment combining in office collaboration and remote working
Competitive time off
Our Flexible Travel Program gives you the option to work abroad in another region/country for up to a month each year

At Ontario Teachers', diversity is one of our core strengths. We take pride in ensuring that the people we hire and the culture we create, reflect and embrace diversity of thought, background and experience. Through our Diversity, Equity and Inclusion strategy and our Employee Resource Groups (ERGs), we celebrate diversity and foster inclusion through events for colleagues to connect for professional development, networking & mentoring. We are building an inclusive and equitable workplace where our talent is respected, accepted and empowered to be themselves. To learn more about our commitment to Diversity, Equity and Inclusion, check out Life at Teachers'.

How To Apply

Are you ready to pursue new challenges and take your career to the next level? Apply today! You may be invited to complete a pre-recorded digital interview as part of your application.

Accommodations are available upon request (peopleandculture@otpp.com) for candidates with a disability taking part in the recruitment process and once hired.

Candidates must be legally entitled to work in the country where this role is located.

Ontario Teachers’ may use AI-based tools to assist in screening and assessing applicants for this position. These tools may help us identify candidates whose skills and experience align with Ontario Teachers’ objectives by analyzing information provided in resumes and applications. Our use of AI does not replace human decision-making.

To learn more about how Teachers’ uses AI with your personal information, please visit our Privacy Centre.

jobs.title: Executive Director, Commercial Products
jobs.description:
Salary Range:
$247,573.00 (Min.) - $334,223.00 (Mid.) - $420,875.00 (Max.)

Required:

At least 10 years of business experience in the insurance/managed health care industry.

At least 10+ years of experience managing staff and leading teams.

Experience working within a matrix management structure to deliver results is critical.

Must have experience in managing multiple products within a commercial health plan.

Must be an experienced manager of people and relish taking on and achieving success on challenging work in a complex company structure.

Experience with outside regulators, governmental entities, etc.

Established in 1997, L.A. Care Health Plan is an independent public agency created by the state of California to provide health coverage to low-income Los Angeles County residents. We are the nation’s largest publicly operated health plan. Serving more than 2 million members, we make sure our members get the right care at the right place at the right time.
Mission: L.A. Care’s mission is to provide access to quality health care for Los Angeles County's vulnerable and low-income communities and residents and to support the safety net required to achieve that purpose.
Job Summary

The Executive Director, Commercial Products (L.A. Care Covered, PASC-SEIU) will take specific responsibility for delivering top and bottom line growth and outstanding compliance and quality score results for a population segment product of the L.A. Care Commercial portfolio. The position is responsible for strategically evaluating, planning and leading complex business initiatives that support the strategic goals of the product strategy and align with enterprise strategy. This position is responsible for directing all aspects of running an efficient team, including hiring, supervising, coaching, training, disciplining, and motivating direct-reports.
This Position Will Oversee The Following Products

Commercial Products:
This position is responsible for the quality, compliance and operational performance of L.A. Care’s commercial products that support the following direct lines of business:
Product: Covered California

L.A. Care Product Name: L.A. Care Covered, L.A. Care Covered Direct
Covered California is the state health insurance exchange for California residents. Their mission is to increase the number of insured Californians, improve health care quality, lower costs, and reduce health disparities through an innovative, competitive marketplace that empowers consumers to choose the health plan and providers that give them the best value. L.A. Care Covered serves eligible residents of Los Angeles County and is positioned for growth through member focused value added programs and services. Members are provided health care and coordinated services through L.A. Care’s contracted network of providers, hospitals, pharmacies and ancillary service providers throughout Los Angeles County.
Product: PASC-SEIU Homecare Workers Health Care Plan

L.A. Care Product Name: PASC-SEIU Plan
L.A. Care offers the PASC-SEIU Homecare Workers Health Care Plan, also known as the PASC-SEIU Plan for eligible In-Home Supportive Services (IHSS) workers, who provide in-home services such as meal preparation and personal care services to Medi-Cal beneficiaries. The Personal Assistance Services Council (PASC), as the employer of record, determines eligibility for PASC-SEIU Homecare Workers Health Care Plan.
Duties

In collaboration with internal key stakeholder partners ensure risk adjustment transfers are minimized and/or medical cost trend targets are met for respective lines of business. Provide leadership to assure the effective and efficient operational management of multiple functions or departments.
Drive for continuous process and product performance improvements and where applicable collaborate with other Executive Directors to work across product lines, applying influence to ensure operational enhancements are developed to be scalable to meet changing market, consumer and provider demands.
Lead respective teams and collaborate with key stakeholders at all levels within the organization to analyze current customer base, market needs and market trends in order to identify potential opportunities for increased efficiencies and capabilities incorporating a scalable scope for development considers the future state. When required, drive end to end product life cycle for new products, program and service offerings across lines of business under a single to market vision and strategy.
Explore potential for additional revenue streams and/or new business development opportunities for products lines where applicable. Where applicable obtain alignment with other Executive Director line-of-business (LOB) owners to create business case and/or discussions for product governance.
Lead the development of annual and long range business plans for respective lines of business. Develop multi-year business plan that helps establish strategic goals of a target population segment product that aligns with overall product vision and enterprise strategic directions.
Operations for product administration and other foundational building blocks that enable the member experience and the delivery of population segment products are provided through organization wide L.A. care departments serving all population segment products. The Executive Director works in close collaboration with cross functional business units across the enterprise to ensure appropriate and timely implementation of federal and state regulatory requirements as well as new products, programs and service initiatives.
Negotiate Service Level Agreements (SLAs) with those organization wide departments for his/her specific population segment product.
Meet and communicate regularly with organization wide operations, non-operations and clinical leaders and work closely with them to resolve issues and manage negative trends with particular emphasis on: Enrollment for accurate and timely loading of members into L.A. Care systems and assignment of members to providers; Timely and accurate claims payments; Member service responsiveness; Network development and provider relations; Data reporting for KPIs and monthly financials; Care management and care coordination; Marketing and sales.
Develops goals, objectives and actions plans for assigned staff which includes full management responsibility for the hiring, performance reviews, salary reviews and disciplinary matters for direct reporting employees.
Performs other duties as assigned.

jobs.title: Managing Director, Private Banker, Palo Alto
jobs.description:
The Private Banker Sr. Principal is an internally and externally recognized subject matter expert that influences the way things are done, not only internally, but beyond the business itself. Requires a broad and comprehensive understanding of the field. Is a true thought leader who develops new techniques, methodologies and approaches in own field to directly impact the work of others and the results of the organization. Recognized as a resource for innovation or groundbreaking developments. Excellent communication skills required in order to negotiate internally and externally at a senior levels. Negotiations will have an impact on the business and are likely to involve external parties. A sizeable impact on a major function and/or the business through making decisions that determine technical approaches and strategies for the area.

Responsibilities:

Actively identify prospects and establish contact with them/convert prospects into clients.
Demonstrate an in-depth understanding of clients’ businesses and sources of wealth generation.
Anticipate events in a client’s life and business which have significant financial implications.
Serve as a trusted advisor to clients who frequently seek strategic advice regarding financial matters.
Build relationships with client families that cross generations to establish long-term connections to the Citi Private Bank.
Effectively manage client expectations regarding what can and cannot be executed on their behalf.
Maintain full understanding of client goals and risk tolerance to ensure that financial strategies are appropriate.
Uncover needs that are not articulated or clearly expressed by the client.
Bring together the product experts to develop long-term solutions to strengthen relationships with the firm.
Help clients achieve broad financial/investment goals related to the creation and management of wealth.
Develop tailored solutions that address clients' investment, risk management, and financing/liquidity needs.
Set challenging goals, regularly monitoring progress and making adjustments to achieve objectives.
Plan and prioritize daily, weekly, and monthly activities to maximize revenue growth and client satisfaction.
Establish a referral network and gain introductions to potential clients which generate new sales.
Close the deal, making concessions that are appropriate and in line with the fundamental interests of the Citi Private Bank.
Help team members acquire the experience necessary to establish credibility with clients and intermediaries.
Demonstrate strong commitment to team success and place team goals above personal achievement.
Encourage team members to work together to deepen client relationships and exceed sales goals.
Align complementary experiences and skill sets of team members to maximize client effectiveness.
Build and maintain relationships with Business Partners, Colleagues and Staff.
Build relationships/partner with support groups in Citi Private Bank to maintain control, find solutions and resolve issues.
Follow standard procedures and advocate appropriate exceptions to standard practices when the bigger picture needs to be considered, and seek or grant approvals as appropriate.
Ensure compliance on regulatory requirements and minimizes any potential liability for Citi.
Ensure strong controls and effective operational and administrative services.
Partner with colleagues across the Citi network to leverage capabilities and share expertise.
Ensure clients are presented with seamless execution of internal processes and Citigroup services.
Prioritize issues that need to be escalated, getting the appropriate people involved to expedite resolution.
Understand and communicate the risk involved with various investment strategies.
Establish credibility and trust with all Citi Private Bank colleagues, maintaining the highest standards of personal and professional integrity.
Persevere in delivering what is promised, even when obstacles arise.
Flag and report illegal or improper transactions, complying with all regulatory policies and control procedures regarding client acceptability.
Understand the broad array of products and services under the Citi umbrella.
Appropriately assess risk when business decisions are made, demonstrating particular consideration for the firm's reputation and safeguarding Citigroup, its clients and assets, by driving compliance with applicable laws, rules and regulations, adhering to Policy, applying sound ethical judgment regarding personal behavior, conduct and business practices, and escalating, managing and reporting control issues with transparency.

jobs.description:
It Starts Here

Santander is a global leader and innovator in the financial services industry and is evolving from a high-impact brand into a technology-driven organization. Our people are at the heart of this journey and together, we are driving a customer-centric transformation that values bold thinking, innovation, and the courage to challenge what’s possible. This is more than a strategic shift. It’s a chance for driven professionals to grow, learn, and make a real difference.

If you are interested in exploring the possibilities We Want to Talk to You!

The Difference You Make

The European Equity Sales Executive Director is responsible for leading the Company's business development efforts throughout the assigned region targeting larger scale clients. The incumbent will drive the growth and retention of existing client relationships, build new client relationships, and provide the full breadth of the Company's products and services to clients and prospects.

Leads the Company’s Equity Research Sales efforts across Europe product targeting institutional clients (long only and hedge funds).
Drives growth and retention of existing client relationships while originating new business opportunities.
Provides the full breadth of the Company’s equities products (including Eq Swaps) and services to clients and prospects.
Acts as a primary client interface delivering market insights, trade ideas, and execution capabilities.
Partners across trading, research, and banking teams to deliver integrated client solutions.
Leads client coverage and sales strategy across European markets.
Builds and maintains relationships with institutional investors, hedge funds, and asset managers.
Partners with team members, including bankers, to uncover cross-sell and syndicated opportunities.
Supports transaction execution, including underwriting processes.
Oversees preparation of marketing materials, client presentations, and roadshow content.
Maintains connectivity with market participants to ensure strong demand and client engagement.

We’re building a relationship-oriented bank for the modern world. We need talented, passionate professionals who are dedicated to doing what’s right for our clients.

At CIBC, we embrace your strengths and your ambitions, so you are empowered at work. Our team members have what they need to make a meaningful impact and are truly valued for who they are and what they contribute.

To learn more about CIBC, please visit CIBC.com

What You’ll Be Doing

We are seeking a highly skilled technologist with deep understanding of the corporate and investment banking business. The primary objective of this role is to support the growth of the business by ensuring our technology systems and processes are fit for purpose, scalable, and aligned with business needs. The successful candidate will collaborate closely with business stakeholders and partner with technology to develop and execute a technology roadmap that bridges business requirements with technological capabilities

Business Planning and Management:

You will help execute Global Corporate and Investment Banking’s (‘GCIB’) strategy, aligning technology initiatives to enable GCIB to achieve its mandate, goals, and objectives. You will set strategic plans that align with GCIB’s overall vision.

Relationship Management & Collaboration:

You will build effective relationships with internal stakeholders to successfully execute initiatives and bring new clients and capabilities to market. You’ll communicate complex concepts clearly and elegantly, collaborating closely with business partners to understand their challenges by asking insightful questions and gathering necessary information. Your ability to demonstrate a deep understanding of business problems will enable you to address any questions or concerns raised by our partners, ensuring alignment and fostering productive collaboration.

How You’ll Succeed

Partner with business leaders in corporate and investment banking to identify strategic technology priorities and requirements.
Assess existing systems and processes, recommending enhancements to drive efficiency, scalability, and compliance.
Partner with technology to develop and maintain a comprehensive technology roadmap aligned to business growth objectives.
Champion AI efforts and business adoption.
Translate business needs into actionable technology solutions, ensuring timely and effective delivery.

Monitor industry trends and emerging technologies relevant to corporate and investment banking.

Develop and execute a comprehensive product strategy for GCIB, ensuring alignment with global business objectives, regulatory requirements, and evolving market trends.
Lead large-scale transformation programs, including digitalization, process re-engineering, and operating model redesign to enhance product offerings and client experience.
Oversee the design, implementation, and governance of data models and taxonomies to support product management, reporting, analytics, and regulatory compliance.
Benchmark and incorporate best practices in product strategy, transformation, and data management from leading global banks.
Collaborate with senior leaders across business lines, technology, risk, operations, and data teams to deliver integrated and scalable product solutions.
Drive market intelligence, including competitor analysis, customer insights, and regulatory developments, to inform product and transformation strategy.
Ensure robust product lifecycle management from ideation through to launch and post-launch optimization, with a focus on data integrity and risk management.
Foster a culture of innovation, data-driven decision making, and continuous improvement within the team.
Manage, mentor, and develop a high-performing team of product strategists, transformation leaders, and data specialists.

Who You Are

Executive Director - Product Strategy & Transformation for Global Corporate & Investment Banking (GCIB) will be responsible for shaping and executing the strategic vision, driving transformational change, and championing digital and data-driven innovation across the GCIB product suite. This executive will lead complex, cross-functional initiatives, including the development of robust data models and taxonomies that underpin product excellence and operational efficiency. The role requires a visionary leader with proven experience in transformation, data strategy, and the ability to influence at the highest levels of a global financial institution.

Bachelor’s degree in Business, Finance, Economics, Data Science, or a related field; MBA or equivalent advanced degree preferred.
12+ years of progressive experience in corporate & investment banking, with a focus on product strategy, transformation, or innovation.

jobs.title: Managing Director, Budgeting
jobs.description:
About Achievement First

Achievement First (AF) is a nonprofit 501(c)(3) organization supporting 39 public charter schools across New York, Connecticut, and Rhode Island. For more than 25 years, AF has been recognized as one of the top tuition-free public charter school networks in the country. We are committed to creating schools of academic excellence within nurturing environments — schools that prepare students to be college-ready and to pursue and succeed in whatever paths they choose in life.

The majority of AF students are Black and Latinx children from low-income families, many of whom will be the first in their families to graduate from college. Across our network, we employ more than 2,200 staff members — 64% of whom identify as Black, Indigenous, or People of Color — who collectively educate nearly 15,000 students in Brooklyn and Queens, NY; New Haven, Bridgeport, and Hartford, CT; and Providence and Cranston, RI. Our annual budget across all of our charters is approximately $350 million.

Our mission is to prepare every student to excel in college, career, and life; deepen their understanding of self and community;
and lead lives of purpose. We believe in the limitless potential within our students and their power to shape a more just society. That belief drives our deep commitment to student achievement: we aim to set a high bar for learning and create warm, demanding classrooms where rigorous instruction is paired with the care, encouragement, and consistency every student deserves.

Your Impact

The Managing Director of Budgeting is responsible for creating, balancing, and maintaining all school budgets and forecasts, as well as supporting the creation and maintenance of Network Support team budgets, overseeing a total budget of approximately $350 million. This includes working directly with schools and regional staff to address budget challenges, identifying and escalating issues to senior leadership, preparing Board reports, and completing all required monthly forecasting tasks.

In addition, this role owns the maintenance and operations of the budgeting and forecasting platform, Workday Adaptive Planning, and provides training and support to budget owners on both budgeting processes and technical system use.

Reporting directly to the Associate Chief of Financial Planning & Analysis, you will be responsible for:

Develop and Lead the School Budget Process

Integrate information from team leads across Network Support, including Executive Team members and Regional Executive Directors, to determine expense budget targets
Incorporate grant funding and other diverse and restricted revenue sources into final budgets
Facilitate budget trade-off discussions for decision making by Regional Executive Directors and the Executive Team, as needed to achieve budget balance
Hold budget meetings with budget owners to review process, answer questions, and ensure team is on track to balance budget for Board approvals
Oversee annual Adaptive Planning (ERP system) budget data loading process
Maintain system functionality, reports, and integration with other systems (e.g. Intacct) and data integrity; amend structure of system as required

Design and Manage School Budget Forecasting

Develop and manage forecasting processes for schools; lead change management on these processes
Communicate directly with schools and engage on process
Lead budget/forecast analysis for each school/team for items trending off from budget and communicate with owners on a monthly basis, at minimum
Plan and manage grant revenues and expenditures with school leaders and grants team
Lead detailed, trend- and actuals-based analysis of school-based forecasts and apply centralized adjustments to improve accuracy for end-of-year results
Manage regional budgets by charter and adjust modified budgets as resources are approved for deployment by Regional Executive Directors
Prepare quarterly Board reports analyzing data as needed
Prepare reporting and communication on budget vs. forecast variance for outside teams (KPI, CEO) and internal (CFO, Associate Chief)

Lead Training of Budget Owners

Design and lead training for budget owners to learn about the process and how to use Adaptive Planning; hold recurring office hours during monthly forecasting and annual budgeting processes
Create and maintain budgeting and forecasting training tools and materials, updating on an annual basis or more frequently as required
Develop, implement, and lead recurring high-level budget training for school leaders and budget owners

Lead and Develop the Team

Manage two Senior Associate level direct reports who support budgeting and forecasting work at the organization level
Mentor staff in conducting 1:1 budget meetings and trainings with budget owners
Provide in-person and hands-on support to senior executives on budget
Facilitate upskilling of school-based leaders on budget and forecasting

Skills You’ll Have

All candidates for this position will demonstrate strong financial acumen, systems proficiency, and the ability to lead complex cross-functional processes in a fast-paced, mission-driven environment.

All candidates must have a Bachelor’s degree (B.A., B.S., etc.); a Finance or Accounting degree is strongly preferred.

An ideal candidate will demonstrate competency in the following areas:

Budgeting, forecasting, and standard accounting practices
Full-cycle budget preparation and management at organizational scale
Workday Adaptive Planning or comparable ERP/budgeting systems
Advanced Excel skills and strong analytical capabilities
KPIs, financial metrics, and variance analysis
Clear and effective written and verbal communication
Training design and facilitation for diverse audiences
Team leadership and staff development

In addition, the following characteristics are required to be successful on this team:

Strong ethics and ability to maintain a high level of confidentiality
Confidence in interacting with and influencing individuals at all levels of the organization
Ability to thrive in a fast-paced, entrepreneurial environment; flexible, able to work autonomously as well as take direction as needed
Exceptional organizational skills, attention to detail, and follow-through
Excellent verbal and written communication skills with a proactive communication style
Belief in the Achievement First mission and educational model

Travel Expectations

This is a hybrid role. The expectation is that the person in the role would live in one of our 3 regions and be able to come into the Network Support office (NYC, CT, or RI) at least 3 days a week. There may be additional travel to regions outside of your own on a monthly basis.

Compensation

Achievement First sets salaries are set through a lens of equity, and based on the requisite skills, education, and experience relevant to the role. The salary for this position is 150,331. In order to uphold our commitment to equity, Achievement First does not negotiate pay. Additionally, Achievement First offers to all regular, full-time employees a comprehensive benefits package that includes paid time off, medical, dental, vision, and life insurance, a 403(b) retirement benefit package with match, and paid Family Leave.

Achievement First is an equal opportunity employer and an organization that values diversity. People from all diverse backgrounds are strongly encouraged to apply. Spanish language proficiency is a plus. You can learn more about diversity at Achievement First here: http://achievementfirst.org/about-us/diversity/

Job Description

THE OPPORTUNITY AT THE DECATUR FAMILY YMCA

The Decatur Family YMCA is seeking a mission-driven, community-centered Chief Executive Officer to lead one of Central Illinois’ long-standing community institutions into its next era of impact, growth, and sustainability. For nearly 150 years, the Decatur Family YMCA has been woven into the fabric of Decatur and Macon County, serving as a trusted gathering place where children discover confidence and possibility, families find support, adults pursue health and wellness, and neighbors of all backgrounds experience connection, belonging, and care.

This is a meaningful leadership opportunity for a visible, relational, and strategic executive who understands both the heart and business of the YMCA. The next CEO will step into an organization with a proud legacy, an engaged volunteer Board of Directors, a committed leadership team, valued community partnerships, and a clear strategic direction shaped by the Board and staff leadership in summer 2025. Building from this foundation, the CEO will have the opportunity to elevate the Y’s community presence, strengthen operational performance, expand philanthropic investment, and tell a more powerful story of the Y’s role in improving lives across Decatur and Macon County.

The ideal leader will bring disciplined executive management, authentic relationship-building, and an entrepreneurial spirit to help the Decatur Family YMCA meet the evolving needs of its community. This is a moment for a leader who can honor the Y’s deep roots while bringing fresh energy, focus, and vision to its future.

About The Decatur Family Ymca

For nearly 150 years, the Decatur Family YMCA has stood as one of Decatur and Macon County’s trusted anchors for youth development, healthy living, and community connection. Rooted in Christian principles and committed to building healthy spirit, mind, and body for all, the Y has earned a meaningful place in the lives of generations of children, families, adults, seniors, volunteers, donors, and community partners.

From its home at 220 W. McKinley Avenue, the Decatur Family YMCA serves as far more than a fitness facility. It is a place where children take their first swim lessons, young people find safe and enriching spaces before and after school, families access trusted childcare, adults pursue healthier lifestyles, seniors remain active and connected, and neighbors find encouragement, belonging, and support. Through preschool, school-age care, Camp Sokkia, aquatics and swim safety, health and wellness, senior wellness, personal training, evidence-based health programs, financial assistance, and community outreach, the Y responds to the evolving needs of the community while staying grounded in its mission.

The Y’s 2026 theme, “No Place Like This Place,” captures the spirit of an organization that is deeply personal to the community it serves. At its best, the Decatur Family YMCA is where programs become relationships, where participation becomes belonging, and where everyday experiences create lasting impact. As the organization looks ahead, it is positioned to build on that trust, sharpen its story, strengthen its reach, and continue serving as a vital community partner for Decatur and Macon County.

Our Mission

To put Christian principles into practice through programs that build a healthy spirit, mind, and body for all.

Our Cause & Values

To strengthen the foundations of community and instill the values of caring, honesty, respect, and responsibility.

Our Culture

The Y Experience – The Y Experience is built from our mission and encompasses the values and philosophy by which we serve and operate
Welcoming – We are open to all. We are a place where you can be yourself and find yourself in others.
Genuine – We value you and embrace your individuality.
Hopeful – We believe in you and your potential to do good in the world.
Nurturing – We support you as you become the best-version-of-yourself.
Determined – Above all else, we are on a relentless quest to make our community stronger, beginning with you

Current Key Strategic Initiatives

Growth - expanding our reach, deepening engagement, and building partnerships to serve more individuals and families. Marketing - elevating the Y’s visibility through consistent messaging and meaningful community connection. Fundraising - ensuring long-term sustainability by strengthening donor relationships and expanding philanthropic support.

About Our Community

Decatur and Macon County offer the next CEO a community defined by pride, partnership, resilience, and possibility. Located in the heart of Central Illinois, Decatur combines the accessibility and affordability of a mid-sized community with the assets, employers, institutions, and civic infrastructure that create meaningful opportunities for impact. The area offers affordable housing, family-friendly neighborhoods, parks and recreation, and convenient proximity to Springfield, Champaign-Urbana, Bloomington-Normal, and key interstate transportation routes.

The region is supported by a diverse economic base that includes agribusiness and food processing, manufacturing, health care, logistics, education, and economic development. Major employers and community assets highlighted in the resource materials include Archer Daniels Midland (ADM), Caterpillar, TCCI, Memorial Health, HSHS St. Mary’s Hospital, Midwest Inland Port, the Economic Development Corporation of Decatur and Macon County, Richland Community College, Millikin University, United Way of Decatur and Mid-Illinois, and a strong network of civic and philanthropic partners.

Life in Decatur is enriched by Lake Decatur, Scovill Zoo, golf courses, parks and trails, arts and civic events, the Devon Lakeshore Amphitheater, local schools and colleges, and a deep tradition of volunteerism and community engagement. For a CEO who wants to be visible, accessible, and meaningfully involved in the life of the community, Decatur offers a powerful setting for leadership. It is a place where relationships are personal, civic pride runs deep, and trusted institutions like the YMCA have an opportunity to bring people together, strengthen families, support health and well-being, and contribute to a stronger future for children, adults, and communities across Macon County.

Fiscally sound business management approach with a proven track record of budgetary and fiscal management of approximately $4M or more annually.
Minimum seven years prior experience in a senior management role with a YMCA or related experience at a comparable organization with direct supervision of multiple levels of staff and employees.
Must be able to develop and strengthen community relationships across civic, business, education, health, nonprofit, philanthropic, and volunteer sectors.
Have the ability to develop innovative programs, review and enhance programs being offered or considered for both economic viability and effectiveness in broadening engagement in the communities served.
Strategic planning and fundraising experience, including annual campaigns, donor stewardship, grants, and partnership development.
Proven ability to lead membership growth, program quality, marketing visibility, and customer/member experience improvements.
A proven record of accomplishment of progressive budgetary leadership and advanced fiscal management practices.
YMCA Organizational Leader Certification or ability to achieve within 3 years.

Essential Functions

KEY SKILLS AND ABILITIES OF OUR NEXT CEO

Role

The board believes its new CEO should be an experienced leader who has the background and qualities that will permit him or her to achieve excellence in the following roles:

Develop and Implement a Strategic Plan:
Work effectively with volunteers, community leaders, and key staff, to create and develop a strategic plan that addresses the needs of the communities served by the Y and includes defined organizational strategies, a clear implementation plan with deliverables, and measurable goals. Key competencies: Change Leadership, Communication & Influence, Critical Thinking & Decision Making, Program/Project Management

Improve Operating Performance:
Engage the board and appropriate staff to develop an annual tactical plan for identifying and achieving key goals and objectives towards operating with safe, relevant, and sustainable practices. Quickly address issues that may arise, forecast future needs, and manage and direct the Y’s operations and activities. Key competencies: Developing Self & Others, Critical Thinking & Decision Making, Innovation

Empower and guide staff leaders to lead their departments to successful financial operation. Develop and implement stewardship strategies. Key competencies: Fiscal Management, Critical Thinking & Decision Making, Developing Self & Others, Philanthropy

Position the Y as a Vital Partner for Community Change:

jobs.title: Co-Founder & Chief Operating Officer (COO)
jobs.description:
About Ukambas
Ukambas is building an AI-powered Business Operating System that transforms fragmented operational information into clear, explainable executive intelligence. Rather than replacing accounting, project-management, CRM, inventory, or field-service platforms, Ukambas is designed to interpret the information generated by those systems and help business leaders understand what requires attention and what decisions should come next.

Our initial market is small and medium-sized HVAC contractors in the United States, including residential, commercial, and service-and-maintenance businesses. The long-term vision is to expand the Operational Intelligence Platform into construction, plumbing, electrical, roofing, manufacturing, logistics, healthcare, and professional services.

The Opportunity
We are looking for a driven, resilient, and business-oriented Co-Founder and Chief Operating Officer
to join Ukambas during its early stage.

This is not a traditional 9-to-5 position. It is a founding partnership for someone prepared to help shape the company, validate the market, develop operating systems, acquire early customers, and scale the business from the ground up.

You will work directly with the Founder and CEO to turn Ukambas’ product vision into a disciplined, customer-focused, and commercially sustainable company.

Key Responsibilities
Strategic leadership:
Co-develop the company strategy, business model, go-to-market plan, milestones, and operating priorities.

Operations:
Build and lead the company’s operational foundation, including internal processes, pilot programs, customer onboarding, performance measurement, and execution discipline.

Customer discovery:
Develop relationships with HVAC owners and executives, understand their workflows and financial challenges, and translate market feedback into clear business requirements.

Go-to-market execution:
Help define Ukambas’ early-access strategy, pilot structure, pricing approach, partnerships, sales process, and customer-success model.

Fundraising:
Partner with the Founder and CEO to prepare pitch materials, develop financial and operational narratives, meet investors, and pursue appropriate funding opportunities.

Team building:
Recruit, lead, and inspire a high-performing early-stage team as the company grows.

Cross-functional leadership:
Connect customer needs, business outcomes, product priorities, and operational execution.

Culture:
Help establish a culture centered on customer value, ownership, transparency, simplicity, continuous improvement, and long-term thinking.

What We Are Looking For
Entrepreneurial grit:
Experience building a business, department, product, operating model, or customer program from the ground up.

Complementary expertise:
Strong experience in one or more of the following areas:

• B2B SaaS operations
• HVAC or construction-industry operations
• Enterprise or contractor-focused sales
• Customer discovery and pilot management
• Business development and strategic partnerships
• Financial planning and fundraising
• Startup go-to-market execution

Business-first thinking:
You understand that technology is a tool for producing better business outcomes—not the objective itself.

Systems thinking:
You can evaluate how people, processes, technology, and financial outcomes influence one another.

Customer focus:
You are willing to spend significant time understanding customers, workflows, operational pain, financial impact, and expected outcomes before recommending solutions.

Commitment:
Ability and willingness to dedicate full-time attention to the venture.

Resilience:
Comfortable operating with limited resources, incomplete information, changing priorities, and the realities of an early-stage startup.

Communication:
Able to communicate complex business challenges clearly to customers, team members, partners, and investors.

Ethical alignment:
Committed to transparency, explainability, responsible AI, data security, and protecting customer trust.

Leadership Alignment
The successful candidate must strongly align with the principles that guide Ukambas:

• Customer value comes first
• Business before technology
• Decisions over reports
• Simplicity wins
• Data requires context
• Trust is built through transparency
• AI assists; humans decide
• Documentation is part of the product
• Long-term sustainability matters more than short-term convenience

At Ukambas, customers do not simply need another dashboard. They need greater confidence in their decisions. Our purpose is to transform operational complexity into clear, explainable intelligence while keeping business leaders fully accountable and in control.

What We Offer
Co-Founder status:
A meaningful equity stake negotiated according to experience, commitment, responsibilities, vesting terms, and long-term contribution.

Strategic influence:
A direct role in shaping the company, operating model, customer strategy, product priorities, and organizational culture.

Operational ownership:
Authority and responsibility to build the business operations and go-to-market organization from the beginning.

Long-term opportunity:
The opportunity to help develop an ambitious platform intended to serve multiple industries and become a trusted source of executive intelligence.

Mission-driven work:
The chance to help business owners spend less time searching for information and more time making confident decisions.

How to Apply
Please submit:

• Your LinkedIn profile or résumé
• A brief introduction describing why you are interested in joining Ukambas as a co-founder
• Examples of businesses, teams, operations, partnerships, or go-to-market programs you have built
• Your availability and expected level of commitment
• The complementary experience you would bring to the founding team

This opportunity is best suited for someone seeking genuine ownership, responsibility, and long-term partnership—not simply a job title.

As the Deputy Director of Budget & Management Services
, you will help shape the County’s direction and guide the work that enables long-term stability and strategic investment. You will work with the Budget Director and senior leadership across the organization, bringing clarity to complex policy and financial issues and strengthening the County’s ability to serve a growing community.

You will lead a talented team of budget managers, performance consultants and budget analysts while cultivating a culture of collaboration, trust, and high performance. This role balances people leadership, strategic financial insight, and the ability to build strong relationships with partners at every level.

In This Role, You Will

Lead major components of the annual budget process and provide analysis that informs the County’s most significant decisions.

Directly supervise three Budget Managers who each supervise a team of budget analysts and performance consultants.

jobs.title: Director of Operations
jobs.description:
Job Details

Description

The Regal Brands family of companies produces decorative hardware products ranging from vintage interior door handle sets to hinges and cabinet hardware, representing a wide range of styles and décor. Our products are sold through various e-commerce platforms such as Amazon, Wayfair, and our own website. We are currently seeking a Director of Operations to lead our warehouse, assembly, shipping, and quality control teams in Denver, Colorado. This position offers an anticipated starting salary of $120,000 - $140,000 annually, plus bonus opportunities, and eligibility for our full benefits package, including health, dental, life, and 401(k).

What You’ll Enjoy

Competitive starting pay

$120,000 - $140,000 per year, salaried, with bonus opportunities.

Comprehensive Benefits Package

Medical, dental, vision, short-term and long-term disability, 401(k), paid holidays, and vacation.

Great work environment

We are a small but established company in the home décor space that invests in the right people. We have enjoyed an established pattern of growth and have no intention of slowing down.

Job Duties And Responsibilities

Reporting to the General Manager, the Director of Operations will provide leadership for approximately 40 employees and three managers across our shipping, logistics, receiving, warehousing, assembly, and quality control teams. This position is responsible for leading multiple department managers while driving continuous improvement efforts to keep our already thriving operation moving forward. This position will be the senior leader for the following departments:

Warehouse shipping, receiving, and assembly/packing teams. This team consists of approximately 20 employees and one manager who are responsible for receiving inventory, storing products, assembling customer orders, and shipping complete orders.
Quality Control team. This team consists of one manager and three employees responsible for inspecting products received from overseas suppliers and ensuring they are free of defects and blemishes before being released into inventory.
Individual contributors, including our Buyer, who is responsible for procuring inventory and managing supplier orders, and our Project Manager, who leads process improvement initiatives.

Other areas of responsibility include but are not limited to:

Overall responsibility for day-to-day operations.
Managing departmental budgets.
Leading, coaching, and developing managers and employees.
Driving continuous improvement initiatives, utilizing our project manager.
Identifying risks early and leading mitigation strategies.
Assisting with new product development.
Ensuring compliance with OSHA and safety standards.

Who We Are Seeking

The ideal candidate is a proven operational leader with extensive experience in e-commerce fulfillment, manufacturing, and supply chain operations. This individual has a track record of building high-performing teams, driving continuous improvement, and scaling operations while maintaining exceptional quality and customer service. Required qualifications are as follows:

10 years of senior leadership experience in e-commerce, fulfillment, manufacturing, or a similar area.
Subject matter expertise in LEAN Manufacturing, Six Sigma, continuous improvement, or a similar area.
Experience with e-commerce, ideally with systems such as Shopify.
Experience creating, managing, and updating Gantt charts.
Experience with project management methodologies, including Agile, Scrum, Waterfall, and new product introduction (NPI) processes.

Who We Are

Regal Brands is home to four premium hardware brands, representing a wide range of styles and décor. Our brands include our flagship, Nostalgic Warehouse, in the Vintage Hardware space, Grandeur Hardware, in the premium Transitional genre, Ageless Iron, a Rustic Farmhouse style, and Viaggio Hardware, our newest entry into the contemporary design arena. Products from our companies are made with quality and durability in mind and our business model nurtures relationships with e-commerce channel partners, as well as offering our own e-commerce purchasing experience through our individual brand websites. We also serve a select number of brick-and-mortar showrooms that represent our products. As a premier door hardware manufacturer, every aspect of growth has been tempered with consideration for the business and consumer communities we serve, based on practices that are fair and sustainable.

new_jobs.title: Director of Development, Campaign Initiatives
new_jobs.description:
Job Summary

The Director of Development for Campaign Initiatives is a front-line fundraising professional responsible for developing and executing strategies to secure philanthropic support for select campaign priorities, multi-disciplinary projects, and non-college specific initiatives across San José State University. Reporting to the Associate Vice President for Development, the Director will provide strategic direction and vision for key fundraising priorities, develop prospect pipelines, and craft compelling cases for support for a variety of fundraising projects and programs. Campaign initiatives for which the incumbent will have primary fundraising responsibility include, but are not limited to, the preservation of Tower Hall, Speed City Legacy Track, Artificial Intelligence, and campaign initiatives from the Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr. Library.

The responsibilities include but are not limited to identifying, qualifying, cultivating, soliciting and stewarding individuals capable of making major and principal gifts to the University. In support of these efforts, the Director maintains and strengthens close working relationships with key academic leaders, campus administrators, various stakeholders and University Advancement colleagues to build consensus around complex funding opportunities.

Key Responsibilities

Manage an assigned portfolio of key prospects and individuals whose capacity and interests align with university-wide strategic initiatives and campaign priorities
Coordinate with Advancement Research and Prospect Research to proactively build and qualify a robust prospect pipeline
Develop and execute complex strategies to identify, cultivate, solicit and steward major gift prospects/donors, including joint solicitations on multi-unit projects
Present and regularly review top prospect strategies with immediate supervisor and other key team members as appropriate
Work collaboratively with university administrators, academic leadership and other stakeholders to gain counsel and offer expertise on donor-centric cultivation and solicitation strategy
Work collaboratively with peers across all development units (Major Gifts, Annual Giving, Planned Giving, Corporate & Foundation Relations) and serve as a key horizontal partner on multi-unit projects
Work with volunteer leadership of university boards, councils and committees to maximize cultivation and solicitation efforts
Focus on securing funding for designated priorities including but not limited to academic programs, endowed chairs and professorships, scholarships, unrestricted support, facility renovations, new facilities and other specified priorities
Collaborate with donor relations colleagues with stewardship efforts to promote a culture of gratitude among internal and external constituents
Develop comprehensive, persuasive proposals for complex, multi-unit funding opportunities, gathering data and buy-in from various campus stakeholder
Effectively utilize campus data management systems and enter prospect/donor contact reports and their relevant data in a timely fashion
Participate in staff, functional and strategy meetings including prospect review sessions, and attend conferences, seminars and other development training as feasible and appropriate

Knowledge, Skills & Abilities

Ability to identify donors and close gifts of six and seven figures
Ability to navigate the complexity of a large, dynamic university and establish consensus among varied stakeholders to make the appropriate match for donor interests
Skilled in networking, high-level prospect discovery and opening doors among new donor segments, coupled with the ability to design and implement sophisticated, donor-centric cultivation and solicitation strategies
Superior ability to successfully manage multiple, large-scale projects with evolving priorities on tight deadlines and under pressure in a dynamic, politically complex and open environment
Exceptional skill in defining problems, gathering and synthesizing complex data, establishing facts and drawing valid conclusions
Exceptional verbal communication and persuasive writing skills
High level of initiative, attention to detail, and organization skills
Proficient in Google Suite and Microsoft Office Suite, and ability to utilize fundraising database programs and learn new tech platforms for project management
Ability to communicate with constituents in a professional and respectful manner

Required Qualifications

Bachelor’s degree from an accredited institution
Minimum of three to five years of progressively responsible experience in fundraising
Experience developing and leading complex solicitation strategies resulting in closing major gifts
Evidence of superior written communication skills; ability to articulate and draft persuasive proposals

Preferred Qualifications

Advanced degree
Experience in identifying, qualifying, and building strategies for new prospects, particularly those with capacity for seven-figure gifts or higher
Fundraising experience within a comprehensive campaign setting at a research or public institution of higher education, specifically managing non-college or cross-disciplinary campaign priorities
Experience serving as a strategic partner to campus administrators and academic leaders

The Opportunity

Ontario Teachers’ Pension Plan (“Teachers’”) manages ~C$270B globally and is recognized as one of the most sophisticated direct investors in the world. With over 80% of assets managed internally, we combine long-term capital with disciplined underwriting and the ability to lead at scale. Teachers’ Venture Growth (TVG) is our dedicated late-stage venture and growth equity platform, managing ~C$15B globally. On the direct investment front, we deploy ~C$1B annually into minority investments in scaled private technology companies. TVG has invested in category leaders such as SpaceX, Databricks, Canva, Anthropic, Gusto, Grafana, Kong, StackAdapt, and many others.

We are seeking a Managing Director in San Francisco
to join our North America Direct team. We are intentionally looking to add a Silicon Valley-based investor who brings differentiated access, cycle-tested judgment, and the ambition to help scale a high-performing institutional growth platform.

The Mandate

You Will

Own sector coverage within North American technology
Develop differentiated investment theses
Originate, lead, and close minority growth investments (typically Series C +)
Write and defend investment committee recommendations
Serve as an active board member or observer
Develop junior talent and elevate team performance

TVG’s average initial equity commitment is ~US$100M and we frequently lead or co-lead rounds. You will collaborate closely with senior TVG leadership, the TVG Funds team, and adjacent OTPP asset classes, but investment decisions and portfolio ownership resides within TVG.

What Success Looks Like

Within the first 12–24 months, you will have:

Expanded our access to high-quality companies and founders not already in our network
Led and closed compelling later-stage investments

new_jobs.title: Managing Director, Budgeting
new_jobs.description:
About Achievement First

Achievement First (AF) is a nonprofit 501(c)(3) organization supporting 39 public charter schools across New York, Connecticut, and Rhode Island. For more than 25 years, AF has been recognized as one of the top tuition-free public charter school networks in the country. We are committed to creating schools of academic excellence within nurturing environments — schools that prepare students to be college-ready and to pursue and succeed in whatever paths they choose in life.

The majority of AF students are Black and Latinx children from low-income families, many of whom will be the first in their families to graduate from college. Across our network, we employ more than 2,200 staff members — 64% of whom identify as Black, Indigenous, or People of Color — who collectively educate nearly 15,000 students in Brooklyn and Queens, NY; New Haven, Bridgeport, and Hartford, CT; and Providence and Cranston, RI. Our annual budget across all of our charters is approximately $350 million.

Our mission is to prepare every student to excel in college, career, and life; deepen their understanding of self and community;
and lead lives of purpose. We believe in the limitless potential within our students and their power to shape a more just society. That belief drives our deep commitment to student achievement: we aim to set a high bar for learning and create warm, demanding classrooms where rigorous instruction is paired with the care, encouragement, and consistency every student deserves.

Your Impact

The Managing Director of Budgeting is responsible for creating, balancing, and maintaining all school budgets and forecasts, as well as supporting the creation and maintenance of Network Support team budgets, overseeing a total budget of approximately $350 million. This includes working directly with schools and regional staff to address budget challenges, identifying and escalating issues to senior leadership, preparing Board reports, and completing all required monthly forecasting tasks.

In addition, this role owns the maintenance and operations of the budgeting and forecasting platform, Workday Adaptive Planning, and provides training and support to budget owners on both budgeting processes and technical system use.

Reporting directly to the Associate Chief of Financial Planning & Analysis, you will be responsible for:

Develop and Lead the School Budget Process

Integrate information from team leads across Network Support, including Executive Team members and Regional Executive Directors, to determine expense budget targets
Incorporate grant funding and other diverse and restricted revenue sources into final budgets
Facilitate budget trade-off discussions for decision making by Regional Executive Directors and the Executive Team, as needed to achieve budget balance
Hold budget meetings with budget owners to review process, answer questions, and ensure team is on track to balance budget for Board approvals
Oversee annual Adaptive Planning (ERP system) budget data loading process
Maintain system functionality, reports, and integration with other systems (e.g. Intacct) and data integrity; amend structure of system as required

Design and Manage School Budget Forecasting

Develop and manage forecasting processes for schools; lead change management on these processes
Communicate directly with schools and engage on process
Lead budget/forecast analysis for each school/team for items trending off from budget and communicate with owners on a monthly basis, at minimum
Plan and manage grant revenues and expenditures with school leaders and grants team
Lead detailed, trend- and actuals-based analysis of school-based forecasts and apply centralized adjustments to improve accuracy for end-of-year results
Manage regional budgets by charter and adjust modified budgets as resources are approved for deployment by Regional Executive Directors
Prepare quarterly Board reports analyzing data as needed
Prepare reporting and communication on budget vs. forecast variance for outside teams (KPI, CEO) and internal (CFO, Associate Chief)

Lead Training of Budget Owners

Design and lead training for budget owners to learn about the process and how to use Adaptive Planning; hold recurring office hours during monthly forecasting and annual budgeting processes
Create and maintain budgeting and forecasting training tools and materials, updating on an annual basis or more frequently as required
Develop, implement, and lead recurring high-level budget training for school leaders and budget owners

Lead and Develop the Team

Manage two Senior Associate level direct reports who support budgeting and forecasting work at the organization level
Mentor staff in conducting 1:1 budget meetings and trainings with budget owners
Provide in-person and hands-on support to senior executives on budget
Facilitate upskilling of school-based leaders on budget and forecasting

Skills You’ll Have

All candidates for this position will demonstrate strong financial acumen, systems proficiency, and the ability to lead complex cross-functional processes in a fast-paced, mission-driven environment.

All candidates must have a Bachelor’s degree (B.A., B.S., etc.); a Finance or Accounting degree is strongly preferred.

An ideal candidate will demonstrate competency in the following areas:

Budgeting, forecasting, and standard accounting practices
Full-cycle budget preparation and management at organizational scale
Workday Adaptive Planning or comparable ERP/budgeting systems
Advanced Excel skills and strong analytical capabilities
KPIs, financial metrics, and variance analysis
Clear and effective written and verbal communication
Training design and facilitation for diverse audiences
Team leadership and staff development

In addition, the following characteristics are required to be successful on this team:

Strong ethics and ability to maintain a high level of confidentiality
Confidence in interacting with and influencing individuals at all levels of the organization
Ability to thrive in a fast-paced, entrepreneurial environment; flexible, able to work autonomously as well as take direction as needed
Exceptional organizational skills, attention to detail, and follow-through
Excellent verbal and written communication skills with a proactive communication style
Belief in the Achievement First mission and educational model

Travel Expectations

This is a hybrid role. The expectation is that the person in the role would live in one of our 3 regions and be able to come into the Network Support office (NYC, CT, or RI) at least 3 days a week. There may be additional travel to regions outside of your own on a monthly basis.

Compensation

Achievement First sets salaries are set through a lens of equity, and based on the requisite skills, education, and experience relevant to the role. The salary for this position is 150,331. In order to uphold our commitment to equity, Achievement First does not negotiate pay. Additionally, Achievement First offers to all regular, full-time employees a comprehensive benefits package that includes paid time off, medical, dental, vision, and life insurance, a 403(b) retirement benefit package with match, and paid Family Leave.

Achievement First is an equal opportunity employer and an organization that values diversity. People from all diverse backgrounds are strongly encouraged to apply. Spanish language proficiency is a plus. You can learn more about diversity at Achievement First here: http://achievementfirst.org/about-us/diversity/

Job Description

THE OPPORTUNITY AT THE DECATUR FAMILY YMCA

The Decatur Family YMCA is seeking a mission-driven, community-centered Chief Executive Officer to lead one of Central Illinois’ long-standing community institutions into its next era of impact, growth, and sustainability. For nearly 150 years, the Decatur Family YMCA has been woven into the fabric of Decatur and Macon County, serving as a trusted gathering place where children discover confidence and possibility, families find support, adults pursue health and wellness, and neighbors of all backgrounds experience connection, belonging, and care.

This is a meaningful leadership opportunity for a visible, relational, and strategic executive who understands both the heart and business of the YMCA. The next CEO will step into an organization with a proud legacy, an engaged volunteer Board of Directors, a committed leadership team, valued community partnerships, and a clear strategic direction shaped by the Board and staff leadership in summer 2025. Building from this foundation, the CEO will have the opportunity to elevate the Y’s community presence, strengthen operational performance, expand philanthropic investment, and tell a more powerful story of the Y’s role in improving lives across Decatur and Macon County.

The ideal leader will bring disciplined executive management, authentic relationship-building, and an entrepreneurial spirit to help the Decatur Family YMCA meet the evolving needs of its community. This is a moment for a leader who can honor the Y’s deep roots while bringing fresh energy, focus, and vision to its future.

About The Decatur Family Ymca

For nearly 150 years, the Decatur Family YMCA has stood as one of Decatur and Macon County’s trusted anchors for youth development, healthy living, and community connection. Rooted in Christian principles and committed to building healthy spirit, mind, and body for all, the Y has earned a meaningful place in the lives of generations of children, families, adults, seniors, volunteers, donors, and community partners.

From its home at 220 W. McKinley Avenue, the Decatur Family YMCA serves as far more than a fitness facility. It is a place where children take their first swim lessons, young people find safe and enriching spaces before and after school, families access trusted childcare, adults pursue healthier lifestyles, seniors remain active and connected, and neighbors find encouragement, belonging, and support. Through preschool, school-age care, Camp Sokkia, aquatics and swim safety, health and wellness, senior wellness, personal training, evidence-based health programs, financial assistance, and community outreach, the Y responds to the evolving needs of the community while staying grounded in its mission.

The Y’s 2026 theme, “No Place Like This Place,” captures the spirit of an organization that is deeply personal to the community it serves. At its best, the Decatur Family YMCA is where programs become relationships, where participation becomes belonging, and where everyday experiences create lasting impact. As the organization looks ahead, it is positioned to build on that trust, sharpen its story, strengthen its reach, and continue serving as a vital community partner for Decatur and Macon County.

Our Mission

To put Christian principles into practice through programs that build a healthy spirit, mind, and body for all.

Our Cause & Values

To strengthen the foundations of community and instill the values of caring, honesty, respect, and responsibility.

Our Culture

The Y Experience – The Y Experience is built from our mission and encompasses the values and philosophy by which we serve and operate
Welcoming – We are open to all. We are a place where you can be yourself and find yourself in others.
Genuine – We value you and embrace your individuality.
Hopeful – We believe in you and your potential to do good in the world.
Nurturing – We support you as you become the best-version-of-yourself.
Determined – Above all else, we are on a relentless quest to make our community stronger, beginning with you

Current Key Strategic Initiatives

Growth - expanding our reach, deepening engagement, and building partnerships to serve more individuals and families. Marketing - elevating the Y’s visibility through consistent messaging and meaningful community connection. Fundraising - ensuring long-term sustainability by strengthening donor relationships and expanding philanthropic support.

Membership

Total Membership Units: 3,930 as of May 2026

Decatur Family YMCA

Health and wellness, group exercise, personal training, senior wellness, and small-group training
Evidence-based health programs including LIVESTRONG at the YMCA, Pedaling for Parkinson’s, Memory Keeper’s Chorus, and arthritis/joint pain support
Y Cares financial assistance, membership assistance, Backpack Attack, child watch, and community outreach

End of May Debt as % of Net Assets = 13%. End of May Total Debt = $857,810.

Last year operating net: ($77,971) for 2025.
Previous year operating net: ($319,029) for 2024.
Current year-to-date operating net: $12,211 as of March 2026.

About Our Community

Decatur and Macon County offer the next CEO a community defined by pride, partnership, resilience, and possibility. Located in the heart of Central Illinois, Decatur combines the accessibility and affordability of a mid-sized community with the assets, employers, institutions, and civic infrastructure that create meaningful opportunities for impact. The area offers affordable housing, family-friendly neighborhoods, parks and recreation, and convenient proximity to Springfield, Champaign-Urbana, Bloomington-Normal, and key interstate transportation routes.

The region is supported by a diverse economic base that includes agribusiness and food processing, manufacturing, health care, logistics, education, and economic development. Major employers and community assets highlighted in the resource materials include Archer Daniels Midland (ADM), Caterpillar, TCCI, Memorial Health, HSHS St. Mary’s Hospital, Midwest Inland Port, the Economic Development Corporation of Decatur and Macon County, Richland Community College, Millikin University, United Way of Decatur and Mid-Illinois, and a strong network of civic and philanthropic partners.

Life in Decatur is enriched by Lake Decatur, Scovill Zoo, golf courses, parks and trails, arts and civic events, the Devon Lakeshore Amphitheater, local schools and colleges, and a deep tradition of volunteerism and community engagement. For a CEO who wants to be visible, accessible, and meaningfully involved in the life of the community, Decatur offers a powerful setting for leadership. It is a place where relationships are personal, civic pride runs deep, and trusted institutions like the YMCA have an opportunity to bring people together, strengthen families, support health and well-being, and contribute to a stronger future for children, adults, and communities across Macon County.
